title: 2x Wood Rolling Shelf
summary: A two-shelf rolling unit built from 2x4 and 2x8 lumber, with black-painted metal pipe alongside the shelves as an accent — a different material system from this catalog's aluminum-angle rolling shelf.
description:
Two 2x4 ladder-style end frames carry the shelves, built from long legs and shorter crosspieces spaced for the two shelf levels — build both ends identically before joining them lengthwise. The shelves themselves are 2x8 boards, run across the frames for real load capacity, unlike a plywood-and-1x setup.

**A length of 1/2" pipe, spray-painted black, mounts alongside the shelves on corner brackets** as a purely cosmetic industrial accent — this is what gives the piece its display-shelf look for a pantry or retail-style setup, and it can be swapped for a different diameter pipe or omitted if the look isn't wanted.

Caster wheels mount to the bottom of the legs last, so the whole shelf rolls. This is a genuinely different build from this catalog's other rolling shelf, which uses aluminum angle iron instead of wood framing — here the wood 2x4/2x8 structure is the whole point.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two end legs at 42", eight cross legs at 39" and four frame connectors at 70" from the 2x4. Cut three shelf boards, narrow ends at 39" and two shelf boards, long at 42" from the 2x8. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to six 12 ft 2x4 and two 12 ft 2x8. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the ladder-frame ends
steps.body: Assemble the two ladder-style end frames from the end legs and cross legs.

steps.title: Join the frames
steps.body: Connect the two end frames lengthwise with the frame connectors.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body: Attach the 2x8 shelf boards across the two shelf levels.

steps.title: Add the pipe accent
steps.body: Paint the pipe black, then mount it alongside the shelves using the corner brackets, if using this detail.

steps.title: Add the casters and finish
steps.body: Mount the caster wheels to the bottom of the legs, then sand and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: 2x wood rolling shelf with black pipe accent
